name: u64_prefix_freedom_external_body
reason: `u64::lemma_serialization_is_not_a_prefix_of` is `external_body` — prefix-freedom for u64 is entirely trusted without proof, despite being provable from `spec_u64_to_le_bytes` fixed-length property

name: usize_prefix_delegates_to_u64_external_body
reason: `usize` prefix-freedom proof delegates to `u64`'s `external_body` lemma — the entire chain of prefix-freedom reasoning for usize rests on an unverified u64 axiom

name: prefix_no_marshalable_guard
reason: `lemma_serialization_is_not_a_prefix_of` has no `is_marshalable` precondition — prefix-freedom is asserted even for values where `ghost_serialize` is only governed by `recommends`, not `requires`

name: view_equal_no_symmetry_enforced
code:
proof fn phi_4_view_equal_no_symmetry_enforced(a: u64, b: u64)
    requires
        a.view_equal(&b),
    ensures
        b.view_equal(&a),
{
}
reason: The trait never requires `view_equal` to be symmetric — an asymmetric implementation could allow `a.lemma_serialization_is_not_a_prefix_of(&b)` to fire in one direction but not the other, creating inconsistent prefix-freedom guarantees

name: u64_fixed_length_trivializes_prefix_freedom
reason: All u64 serializations have the same length (8 bytes), making the prefix-freedom lemma's `len() <= len()` precondition always true — the "not a prefix" guarantee reduces to simple injectivity, yet this is hidden behind an `external_body` that claims a stronger property than needed
